prevalence.value: 0.3-1.0% (any cause; varies by inpatient vs outpatient)

burden.mortalityRate: Low overall; higher in severe secondary causes

epidemiology.diagnosticCriteria: Platelets <150,000/µL with evaluation for pseudothrombocytopenia, smear review, and secondary cause assessment

publicHealthSystem.screeningCoverage: CBC detection common in routine and inpatient labs
publicHealthSystem.estimatedPatients: millions with transient or chronic thrombocytopenia
publicHealthSystem.controlRate: Generally good with access to hematology, immunotherapy, and transfusion services

regionalSpecificities.diagnosisCriteria: High access to hematology and diagnostic workups in most member states
regionalSpecificities.treatmentProtocol: Etiology-specific; ITP treatments (steroids, IVIG, thrombopoietin receptor agonists) and transfusion support when indicated
regionalSpecificities.regionalChallenges: Variation in access to high-cost agents between countries
regionalSpecificities.strongPoints: Robust diagnostic and transfusion infrastructure in many systems
